name: KNIME publication and workflow reproducibility audit script chain
purpose: Formal workflow index for the article and workflow audit process, from OpenAlex article collection through article-audit table generation.

handoff_terms.automatic: The next step consumes files written by this step without required manual editing.
handoff_terms.semi_automatic: The script produces or consumes an intermediate state that may require manual action, such as institutional article download, human article assessment, workflow retrieval, or manual KNIME opening/execution checks.
handoff_terms.llm_automatic: The script runs automatically after local preprocessing, but uses an LLM for bounded judgment tasks that previously required human review.
handoff_terms.manual: The step is not currently represented by a deterministic script and must be performed or edited by a human before the next scripted step is meaningful.

article_audit_chain.name: Collect OpenAlex KNIME article records
article_audit_chain.notes: Raw OpenAlex records are the source for bibliometric summaries and rank-based article download attempts.

article_audit_chain.name: Build OpenAlex bibliometric summaries and most-cited seed table
article_audit_chain.notes: The current Makefile default writes the top 100 citation-ranked seed table, matching the current structured assessment scope.

article_audit_chain.name: Attempt article PDF downloads by citation-rank range
manual_action_required: Inspect failed, non-PDF, or paywalled download attempts.
Download articles manually where institutional access is available and lawful.
Place manually obtained PDFs under data/original/articles/.
article_audit_chain.notes: This step is semi-automatic because OpenAlex links do not always provide retrievable PDFs and some articles require university subscription access.

article_audit_chain.name: Extract semantic article HTML with GROBID
input: local GROBID service: docker run --rm --init --ulimit core=0 -p 8070:8070 grobid/grobid:0.9.0-crf
article_audit_chain.notes: The only article parser in the current pipeline. Uses GROBID processFulltextDocument to create TEI XML, then renders semantic HTML with title, abstract, body sections, paragraphs, and bibliography. The full local run processed 83 PDFs with 0 failures. Use article-grobid-html-all to regenerate all outputs.

article_audit_chain.name: Collect article URLs and metadata
article_audit_chain.notes: Current article-to-workflow analysis starts here. This step joins the OpenAlex seed table, canonical article registry, and processed GROBID HTML/TEI. It collects article metadata and normalized article-related URLs only. It does not infer audit flags, classify URLs, call URLs, read the curated audit, or use an LLM. The registry and full DOI-derived keys remain the source of truth for local article identity.

article_audit_chain.name: Fetch referenced URL pages
article_audit_chain.notes: Fetches each unique collected URL, records HTTP status, redirect chain, final URL, headers, and stored response body. It does not classify pages or infer workflow availability.

article_audit_chain.name: Attach URL fetch metadata to article records
article_audit_chain.notes: Adds fetch status, HTTP code, final URL, redirect count, and stored page paths to each URL entry. This is metadata attachment only, not URL interpretation.

article_audit_chain.name: Browser-fetch HTTP 403 reference pages
manual_action_required: Do not solve captchas or bypass access controls.
Treat myexperiment.org as captcha-protected and record blocked_by_challenge rather than repeatedly visiting it.
Review browser_fetch_status values for failed downloads or challenge pages before interpreting workflow access.
article_audit_chain.notes: Second-stage retrievability check for URLs that simple HTTP fetching could not access. Uses Playwright/Chrome where useful, saves rendered HTML, and records challenge pages explicitly.

article_audit_chain.name: Classify reference pages for workflow obtainability
manual_action_required: Run by explicit rank ranges when testing or extending the audit, for example --rank-from 41 --rank-to 100.
Review manual_check_required records before treating them as confirmed workflow availability.
Review KNIME Hub extension/documentation pages separately from actual KNIME Hub workflow pages.
article_audit_chain.notes: This is the preferred current evidence path for article-to-workflow relations. The LLM receives only URL metadata and locally cached reference-page excerpts, not the full article text. Empirically this gives a clearer signal about whether the article reference lets a reader obtain a KNIME workflow than article-text-only LLM analysis. The output supports article-level workflow-reference counts and prioritizes manual workflow retrieval.

article_audit_chain.name: Create or update workflow-reference inventory
manual_action_required: Retrieve workflow archives, workflow directories, repository snapshots, or supplements where possible.
Open retrieved workflows in the local current KNIME environment.
Record import/opening outcome, execution outcome, screenshots, missing extensions, missing data, and other failure notes.
article_audit_chain.notes: Human semi-automatic work now starts from data/processed/audit/article_reference_llm_classifications.json. Inspect records with reference_type values such as knime_hub_workflow, myexperiment_workflow, workflow_repository, possible_workflow_supplement, and records where workflow_access is direct_workflow_available, workflow_landing_page_available, possible_workflow_requires_inspection, or manual_check_required. Follow the recorded URLs manually in a browser or with a download tool when allowed. Save obtained workflow archives, workflow directories, HTTP traces, and screenshots under data/original/workflows/<article-or-doi-safe-directory>/. Update the workflow-reference inventory with retrieval status, downloaded files, workflow files found, failed references, directory, and notes. Open retrieved workflows in the current local KNIME environment when feasible, then update manual KNIME opening tests. If nothing is obtained, record the reason explicitly rather than deleting the reference.

article_audit_chain.name: Build article-audit summary table source
article_audit_chain.notes: Use --fail-on-mismatch when table values should match the manuscript exactly.

article_audit_chain.name: Build KNIME-use workflow-reporting table source
article_audit_chain.notes: Uses the workflow inventory summary count for articles with successfully downloaded workflows.

out_of_scope_for_current_scripts: Final VWRS weighting function f(A, I, E, M, D, X, C, R)
Fully automatic article PDF retrieval through institutional subscription access
Fully automatic workflow artifact retrieval from every article-specific repository or portal
Fully automatic KNIME workflow execution and repair
